video:video.video:title: How Tessa Thompson got Janelle Monáe to star in 'Is God Is'
video:video.video:description: At the New York premiere of "Is God Is," producer Tessa Thompson talks about getting Janelle Monáe to star in the revenge thriller, while actor Kara Young gushes over bonding with her co-stars.

video:video.video:title: Nonprofit sends trainers nationwide in effort to prevent school shootings after Sandy Hook killings
video:video.video:description: A group formed by relatives of victims of the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting has sent instructors around the country to teach students ways to prevent such violence in their communities.

video:video.video:title: Invasive species: Colombians divided over plans to cull hippos linked to Pablo Escobar
video:video.video:description: Along the Magdalena River, one of Colombia’s primary arteries, fishermen move with wary precision - hippopotamuses can surface without warning, closing the distance to a vessel in a single heartbeat.

video:video.video:title: A growing amateur choir brings joy and community to hundreds in Serbia
video:video.video:description: An amateur pop choir that started with a couple dozen singers in a small Serbian town has found unexpected success across the country. Its motto? Anyone can sing.

video:video.video:title: 8,000-year-old wheat grains in Georgia show the earliest evidence of breadmaking
video:video.video:description: A small imprint left on a piece of Neolithic mudbrick in Georgia is helping rewrite the story of one of the world’s most important crops.

video:video.video:title: US will issue commemorative passports with Trump’s picture for America's 250th birthday
video:video.video:description: The State Department says it's preparing a limited release of commemorative U.S. passports celebrating America’s 250th birthday and featuring a picture of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Business basics: How do interest rates impact me?
video:video.video:description: Interest rates affect how much people spend when they borrow to buy a home, car, or large appliance. Yet different forces can affect those rates

video:video.video:title: How toxic runoff from rare earth mines are risking Southeast Asia's rivers
video:video.video:description: The Mekong River, that runs through six countries, has long faced mounting pressures, from plastic pollution to hydropower dams hemming it upstream and sand mining devouring its banks.

video:video.video:title: Trump says King Charles 'agrees with me' about not letting Iran have a nuclear weapon
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Britain's King Charles III "agrees with me even more than I do," about not letting Iran have a nuclear weapon.

video:video.video:title: The Gathering of Nations Powwow comes to an end in New Mexico
video:video.video:description: This year marked the final Gathering of Nations. The event started more than 40 years ago and grew into what organizers billed as the largest powwow in North America.

video:video.video:title: EU urges tourists to not postpone travel and holiday plans during fuel crisis
video:video.video:description: The European Union urged tourists and travelers on Wednesday to go ahead with their holiday and travel plans, saying that there were no jet fuel shortages and that they would be protected should any disruption occur.

video:video.video:title: Shoplifting meets social satire at 'I Love Boosters' premiere in Oakland
video:video.video:description: The West Coast premiere of "I Love Boosters" brought Boots Riley and a star-studded cast to Oakland, where the hometown director chose to debut his latest film.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories for April 29
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 29th: Outrage over stabbings in London; Chief financial officer for the Pentagon puts cost of war with Iran at $25 billion so far; Federal agents serve warrants on child care centers in Minneapolis; Tamarin and anteater form a bond at Hungary zoo

video:video.video:title: Trump welcomes Supreme Court Voting Rights Act ruling as the 'kind of ruling I like'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says says more congressional districts should be redrawn after Wednesday's Supreme Court ruling.

video:video.video:title: Massachusetts weather station shows the science behind our climate
video:video.video:description: At Blue Hill Observatory and Science Center near Boston, staff and volunteers have been using many of the same tools to monitor the weather since it opened in 1885.

video:video.video:title: UN nuclear chief says majority of Iran’s highly enriched uranium is still believed to be at Isfahan complex
video:video.video:description: The head of the U.N. nuclear agency says the majority of Iran’s highly enriched uranium is still believed to be at its Isfahan nuclear complex.

video:video.video:title: Enjoy the Kentucky Derby with a homemade mint julep
video:video.video:description: Angelos Bafas, bartender and co-founder of cato bar in London, demonstrates how to make a classic mint julep ahead of Kentucky Derby weekend. (April 29)

video:video.video:title: Scene outside Supreme Court ahead of TPS arguments
video:video.video:description: Scene outside Supreme Court ahead of TPS arguments

video:video.video:title: A barge carrying Timmy the humpback whale begins journey to the North Sea
video:video.video:description: A barge carrying a stranded humpback whale has started its journey toward the North Sea. (Produced by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: The UAE’s departure from OPEC shakes up the alliance that influences oil prices worldwide
video:video.video:description: The UAE will leave OPEC effective Friday, stripping the oil cartel of its third-largest producer and further weakening its leverage over global oil supplies and prices.

video:video.video:title: Defying protocol, Trump relays details of private conversation with King Charles III
video:video.video:description: In the world of diplomatic faux pas it could have been a lot worse. U.S. President Donald Trump said at Tuesday’s state dinner honoring King Charles III and Queen Camilla that during a private meeting earlier in the day the British monarch had agreed with him that Iran should never be allowed to hav

video:video.video:title: James Comey indicted over '86 47' seashell photo officials call Trump threat
video:video.video:description: Former FBI Director James Comey has been indicted again, this time over a social media photo of seashells arranged on a beach that officials say constituted a threat against President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Meet the Liberty Bell fans visiting little-known replicas scattered across the country
video:video.video:description: For a 1950 bond drive, the U.S. Treasury Department commissioned replicas for each U.S. state and several territories, and there's a group of bell hunters out there trying to visit and ring as many as they can.

video:video.video:title: America's 250th: The Liberty Bell
video:video.video:description: Want to see the Liberty Bell as America celebrates its 250th birthday but don’t have the time or cheese for a pilgrimage to Philadelphia?

video:video.video:title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla honor victims at 9/11 Memorial in NYC on the third day of US trip
video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla visit the National 9/11 Memorial in New York City, honoring victims of the 2001 attacks.

video:video.video:title: London police say stabbing of 2 Jewish men has been declared a terrorist incident
video:video.video:description: Two Jewish men were stabbed and injured in a London street on Wednesday, in what police called an act of terrorism.

video:video.video:title: Democrats investigate as Trump OKs almost $2 billion in taxpayer money to end offshore wind projects
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is spending nearly $2 billion to get energy companies to walk away from U.S. offshore wind projects. Democrats in Congress are investigating.

video:video.video:title: White House says funds to pay TSA and other Homeland Security workers will ‘soon run out’
video:video.video:description: The White House is warning Congress that funding to pay Department of Homeland Security personnel will "soon run out,” sparking new threats of airport disruptions and national security concerns.

video:video.video:title: Immigrants rally outside Supreme Court arguments on bid to end protections for Haitians, Syrians
video:video.video:description: Dozens of immigrants who have been protected from deportation under a temporary status gathered in front of the Supreme Court to rally against the Trump administration’s attempt to end those protections for Haitians and Syrians.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Hegseth slams Iran war critics as the 'biggest adversary' in heated hearing
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at lawmakers who have criticized the Iran war, calling them the “biggest adversary we face,” as he defended the conflict before a heated House Armed Services Committee hearing Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Haitian community leader in Ohio hopes SCOTUS does not remove protected status
video:video.video:description: Rose-Thamar Joseph listened to arguments at the US Supreme Court as justices weighed the Trump administration’s push to end legal protections for Haitians and Syrians as migrants fleeing war and natural disaster.

video:video.video:title: Trump to be featured on commemorative US passports for America 250
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump will appear on commemorative U.S. passports for America’s 250th anniversary, becoming the first living president featured in the document.

video:video.video:title: 'I think it's great:' Trump cheers UAE's plan to exit OPEC
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump praised the United Arab Emirates' decision to leave OPEC on May 1, saying it could ease oil prices and calm markets rattled by the Iran war, calling the move “a good thing” for lowering gas and energy costs.

video:video.video:title: What to know about The Onion's new effort to take over Alex Jones' Infowars
video:video.video:description: The Onion is taking another stab at getting control of Alex Jones' Infowars platforms and turning them into comedy sites spoofing the conspiracy theorist.

video:video.video:title: Skeptical Democrats confront Hegseth about Iran war for the first time since conflict started
video:video.video:description: A hearing before the House Armed Services Committee is focused on the Trump administration's $1.5 trillion military budget for 2027, but Democrats are using the opportunity to grill the Secretary of Defense on the Iran war’s costs.

video:video.video:title: 'A very beautiful and moving' British royal visit to 9/11 Memorial cheers victims' family members
video:video.video:description: The visit of King Charles III and Queen Camilla to the National 9/11 Memorial, where they honored victims of the 2001 attacks, was warmly received.

video:video.video:title: Texas tornado leaves 5 injured, buildings collapsed and homes without roofs
video:video.video:description: Officials have confirmed a tornado with winds of at least 120 mph tore through a small Texas city where manufacturing buildings got flattened and roofs were torn from homes.

video:video.video:title: Federal agents raid day care centers across Minneapolis in fraud investigation
video:video.video:description: Federal agents served multiple search warrants Tuesday in Minnesota in an ongoing fraud investigation by the Trump administration of publicly funded social programs, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Colombians divided over plans to cull hippos linked to Pablo Escobar
video:video.video:description: Along the Magdalena River, one of Colombia’s primary waterways, fishermen move with wary precision as hippos introduced by Pablo Escobar in the 1980s have become a major safety issue.

video:video.video:title: Federal Reserve keeps key rate unchanged; four officials dissent, most in almost 34 years
video:video.video:description: The Federal Reserve left its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 3.6% for the third straight meeting.

video:video.video:title: In a drought? Here's how to grow your own food with less water
video:video.video:description: The U.S. West is already seeing outdoor watering restrictions after one of its worst snow years on record.

video:video.video:title: Lindsey Vonn 'not in a position emotionally' yet to decide on racing again after Olympic ski crash
video:video.video:description: Lindsey Vonn is still recovering physically and emotionally from her frightening crash at the Winter Olympics. if the 41-year-old Vonn races again — and she’s not ready to make that decision — she thinks a return is at least a year and a half away.

video:video.video:title: In a drought? Here's how to grow food with less water
video:video.video:description: In drought-prone regions, watering your vegetable garden is typically allowed even when watering lawns is restricted.

video:video.video:title: Timeline of Minnesota fraud investigations
video:video.video:description: Minnesota has been beset by a series of complex, overlapping fraud investigations that have stretched over several years and involve potentially billions of dollars in wasted federal funding.

video:video.video:title: Kenya gives a hero's welcome to marathon record breaker Sabastian Sawe
video:video.video:description: Kenyan marathon record-breaker Sabastian Sawe received a hero's welcome upon his return to Nairobi on Wednesday after winning the London Marathon.

video:video.video:title: Florida Senate passes new redistricting bill intended to boost Republicans in midterms elections
video:video.video:description: The Florida Legislature approved a new congressional map on Wednesday intended to help Republicans gain four seats. The vote came the same day that the U.S.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Skylar Grey relives childhood in new album inspired by turning 40
video:video.video:description: Skylar Grey says her new album “Wasted Potential” was inspired by turning 40 and looking to "relive my childhood.” (April 29)

video:video.video:title: Mariah the Scientist on fan connection: 'We’re just a unit'
video:video.video:description: Speaking at Billboard Women in Music, Mariah the Scientist spoke about her audience appreciating her for wearing her heart on her sleeve. (April 29)

video:video.video:title: Migrants line up overnight for Spain's "extraordinary regularization" program
video:video.video:description: Migrants in Spain are facing hours-long lines and in some cases are sleeping on the streets overnight for a chance at the country's "extraordinary regularization" scheme.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III visits urban farming program, Queen Camilla visits the New York Public Library
video:video.video:description: Charles toured the local nonprofit organization Harlem Grown and even fed their chickens and Camilla visited the famed library, where she was to deliver a Roo doll for the library’s collection of Winnie-the-Pooh stuffed animals as the children’s character turns 100.

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage released as London police say stabbing of two Jewish men was act of terrorism
video:video.video:description: London's Metropolitan Police on Wednesday released video from the body-worn cameras of two officers involved in detaining a man accused of stabbing two Jewish men on a London street in the city's latest antisemitic attack.

video:video.video:title: Exonerees face roadblocks in the journey from prison to employment
video:video.video:description: People who are wrongfully incarcerated then exonerated, sometimes after spending decades behind bars, face yet more challenges finding jobs and rebuilding their lives after their release.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese father questions Israeli warning after strike kills wife and daughter
video:video.video:description: Lebanese father Hussein Farran has questioned Israel's warning system, saying it's "meaningless" after his wife and daughter were killed in an Israeli strike in Kfar Hatta village in southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Dionne Warwick talks final album, Teyana Taylor portraying her in biopic
video:video.video:description: Speaking at Billboard Women in Music, Dionne Warwick calls Teyana Taylor her “alter ego” after handpicking her to portray her in a planned biopic. (April 29)

video:video.video:title: Countries end Colombia fossil fuel summit with focus on next steps and financing
video:video.video:description: Participating countries wrapped up a first-of-its-kind summit in Colombia on phasing out fossil fuels, with no binding commitments, but growing momentum to shift from pledges to action.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Mamdani says he would encourage King Charles to ‘return the Koh-i-Noor diamond’
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani on Wednesday said that if he were to speak with King Charles III separately of an event honoring the victims of 9/11, he would "probably encourage the King to return the Koh-i-Noor diamond.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 30
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday April 30th: Supreme Court strikes down Louisiana majority Black congressional district; Defense Secretary Hegseth to testify before Senate Committee; Court filings say Correspondents Dinner shooting suspect took a picture of himself before the attack; King Charles III
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Lawmakers blast and praise Hegseth over Iran war during congressional hearing
video:video.video:description: A congressional hearing that was supposed to center on President Donald Trump's 2027 military budget proposal has entered its fifth hour, with Democrats and Republicans oscillating between praise and condemnation for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

video:video.video:title: French teen charged in Singapore over vending machine straw-licking video
video:video.video:description: A French teen faces charges in Singapore after posting a video of himself licking a straw from an orange juice vending machine and putting it back.

video:video.video:title: Black Louisiana congressman reacts to Supreme Court ruling on race-based redistricting
video:video.video:description: On Wednesday, the Supreme Court weakened a key tool of the Voting Rights Act that has helped combat racial discrimination in voting for over 50 years.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Trump meets Artemis II crew at the White House, says US has 'good shot' at returning to the moon
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump met with the crew of Artemis II at the White House Wednesday. He also said the U.S. has a 'good shot' of landing an astronaut on the moon before his term is over.

video:video.video:title: 200 years of London Zoo: From Charles Darwin to David Attenborough
video:video.video:description: London Zoo is celebrating its 200th birthday by announcing the development of a new wildlife health center thanks to the largest single donation in its history of £20 million.

video:video.video:title: Beloved statue of Nipper the dog faces uncertain future
video:video.video:description: A 28-foot tall white dog with black ears, known as Nipper, has sat atop a warehouse in Albany for almost seven decades.

video:video.video:title: The future of Nipper the dog statue in Albany is up in the air
video:video.video:description: The 28-foot statue of the famous RCA dog listening to a phonograph has become a local icon in Albany.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court voids majority Black congressional district in Louisiana, boosting Republican chances
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has struck down Louisiana’s second majority Black congressional district in a decision that could open the door for Republican-led states to eliminate Black and Latino electoral districts that tend to favor Democrats.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Japan Airlines to trial humanoid robots at Haneda airport
video:video.video:description: At Tokyo's Haneda Airport, a new kind of worker will take on some tasks next month. Japan Airlines and a robotic company announced that they will test humanoid robots next month to potentially help with handling baggage, cleaning cabins, and operating ground support equipmen

video:video.video:title: Forget the roads, the post takes to the water as late spring reaches northern Germany
video:video.video:description: Andrea Bunar had been waiting for months for the arrival of spring and the day she could finally delivery the mail by barge again through the narrow rivers and creeks of the Spreewald Forest Delta southeast of Berlin.

video:video.video:title: Jewish community condemns attack after 2 stabbed in a Jewish neighborhood of London
video:video.video:description: Police arrested a 45-year-old man on suspicion of attempted murder in the city's latest antisemitic attack.

video:video.video:title: Fed Chair Powell warns of 'unprecedented' legal attacks on Fed by Trump administration
video:video.video:description: Jerome Powell said Wednesday he plans to remain on the board of the Federal Reserve after his term as chair ends next month.

video:video.video:title: Mississippi middle school students stop bus from crashing after driver blacks out
video:video.video:description: Middle school students in Mississippi kept their school bus from crashing after their driver passed out while on a four-lane highway.

video:video.video:title: Honorees arrive for Billboard Women in Music Awards 2026
video:video.video:description: Teyana Taylor, HUNTR/X and Dionne Warwick are honored at Billboard Women in Music.

video:video.video:title: In Tennessee, solar developers explore benefits of cattle and energy production
video:video.video:description: A solar company is testing how cattle and solar panels can better co-exist at a small farm in central Tennessee and what the potential benefits might be for farmers, the energy industry and the land.

video:video.video:title: Kenya's marathon record breaker Sabastian Sawe receives hero's welcome on return home
video:video.video:description: Marathon record-breaker Sabastian Sawe has received a hero's welcome in Kenya, meeting with President William Ruto in a special ceremony on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices whipsaw while US stocks glide near their record heights
video:video.video:description: The S&P 500 rose 0.2% and inched closer to its all-time high set earlier this week and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 342 points, or 0.7%, as of 9:35 a.m.

video:video.video:title: Explosion and fire collapses home in New York amid domestic dispute
video:video.video:description: New York police search for a suspect after a house collapsed in a massive explosion and fire in Queens; appears to have started as a domestic dispute

video:video.video:title: Iran's supreme leader says it will protect its nuclear and missile capabilities
video:video.video:description: Iran's supreme leader said Thursday that the Islamic Republic will protect its “nuclear and missile capabilities” as a national asset, likely seeking to draw a hard line as U.S.

video:video.video:title: Countries discuss how to move away from fossil fuels at international conference
video:video.video:description: Countries have wrapped up a first-of-its-kind summit in Colombia on phasing out fossil fuels with no binding commitments but a growing momentum to shift from pledges to action.

video:video.video:title: Elon Musk arrives at courthouse for OpenAI trial in California
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk, the Tesla CEO, world’s richest man and OpenAI’s cofounder, took the stand Tuesday in a high-stakes trial revolving around a bitter feud between himself and former friends Sam Altman and Greg Brockman that could reshape the future development of artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: The Netherlands boosts its reservists, aided by royal recruitment
video:video.video:description: The Dutch government and top brass have committed to raising military personnel from its current 80,000 to 120,000 by 2035 — plans that have broad political support.

video:video.video:title: Media organizations call on Israel to allow foreign reporters independent access to Gaza
video:video.video:description: Leaders of major media companies are urging Israel to lift a ban preventing foreign journalists from independently entering Gaza.

video:video.video:title: The first direct US-Venezuela commercial flight in 7 years leaves Miami
video:video.video:description: The first direct commercial flight between the United States and Venezuela departed Thursday on its way to the capital of the South American country, seven years after flights were suspended.

video:video.video:title: Dan Levy reflects on his days as an MTV reporter
video:video.video:description: Dan Levy gives credit to red carpet journalists at the New York premiere of his latest series, "Big Mistakes." He was a host for MTV Canada for almost a decade.

video:video.video:title: Pier pressure! Massive Steller sea lion ‘Chonkers’ makes a splash in San Francisco
video:video.video:description: A giant Steller sea lion nicknamed "Chonkers" has unexpectedly arrived at Pier 39 in Fisherman’s Wharf, drawing crowds as he lounges among the much smaller resident sea lions.

video:video.video:title: First footage of wild cougar with kittens captured in Minnesota
video:video.video:description: Previously extinct in Minnesota, the video is the first documented evidence that cougars are reproducing in the state in more than 100 years according to the Department of Natural Resources.

video:video.video:title: Queen Camilla buys Thin Mint Girls Scout cookies at Virginia block party
video:video.video:description: When Front Royal Virginia Mayor Lori Cockrell received a voicemail from the British Embassy inquiring about a visit from the King of England to her small town, she assumed it was spam; as it turns out, it was a very real call.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 30
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday April 30: Terror threat level raised in the UK; House ends Homeland Security shutdown; Trump makes new pick for surgeon general; First direct U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's lifting certain tariffs on Scotch whisky
video:video.video:description: The president said the tariffs were lifted to specifically enhance the trade of barrels between Scotland and Kentucky, which produces almost all of the world's bourbon.

video:video.video:title: Social media trend has young people 'speedrunning' through the Church of Scientology
video:video.video:description: A growing social media trend has young people "speedrunning" through Church of Scientology buildings on Hollywood Boulevard in Los Angeles, prompting the church to boost security and remove its external door handles.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III leaves after 4-day state visit to the US
video:video.video:description: Britain's King Charles III left the United States on Thursday after a four-day state visit. He walked across the tarmac and on to a red carpet, past the Honor Guard, before boarding his flight for Bermuda.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla visit Front Royal, Virginia
video:video.video:description: The King and Queen joined residents at a neighborhood block party in Front Royal, Virginia, celebrating America’s upcoming 250th anniversary. (AP Video: Nathan Ellgren)

video:video.video:title: Mississippi Democrats fear weakening Voting Rights Act will cost them recent gains
video:video.video:description: Mississippi State Sen. Johnny DuPree was elected after his district was redrawn to correct a violation of the Voting Rights Act. Now, after the U.S.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Lindsey Vonn tells the AP she’s not ready emotionally to decide if she’ll race again
video:video.video:description: Lindsey Vonn says she’s not ready emotionally to decide if she’ll race again after crashing just seconds into her downhill run at the Winter Olympics, suffering a severe leg injury that required multiple surgeries.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of residents in southern Lebanon mourn 3 paramedics killed in Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of mourners marched on Thursday behind the coffins of three Lebanese Civil Defense paramedics killed by Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Sheinbaum seeks 'clear evidence' over charges against Mexican officials in US indictment
video:video.video:description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said on Thursday that she wouldn't let the U.S. meddle in the country's affairs, and that her attorney general would investigate allegations from a New York court indictment accusing 10 Mexican current and former officials of working with the Sinaloa Cartel to tra

video:video.video:title: Bodycam video shows moment house explodes as NYPD officers respond to call
video:video.video:description: Body camera footage shows the moment a New York City home exploded as police officers responded to a domestic dispute call.

video:video.video:title: Britain's King Charles III arrives in Bermuda
video:video.video:description: Britain's King Charles III arrived in Bermuda on Thursday on his first visit to a British overseas territory as King.

video:video.video:title: The first direct US-Venezuela commercial flight in 7 years lands in Caracas
video:video.video:description: The first direct commercial flight between the United States and Venezuela arrived Thursday in the capital of the South American country, seven years after the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Iran’s rial currency hits record low as US naval blockade continues
video:video.video:description: Iran’s national rial currency has dropped to a record low while a U.S. naval blockade has increased pressure on its already battered economy amid a fragile ceasefire.

video:video.video:title: Long DACA renewal wait times leave some 'Dreamers' without status, a job and fearing detainment
video:video.video:description: Renewal wait times for the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals program that allows people who were brought to the U.S. as children to temporarily remain in the country and work have increased dramatically in the past year.

video:video.video:title: What to know as May Day turns 140
video:video.video:description: As activists worldwide kick off May Day rallies Friday, demanding peace, higher wages, and better working conditions, its 140 year history shows an evolving focus beyond workers' rights.

video:video.video:title: Habitat for Humanity is developing a new Atlanta community with help from the Carters' initiative
video:video.video:description: In only five days, nearly 1,000 volunteers with Habitat for Humanity will complete 24 affordable housing units in Atlanta's Sylvan Hills neighborhood.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents’ dinner
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities said a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner and attempt to kill U.S.

video:video.video:title: Stanley Tucci and Emily Blunt receive stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Meryl Streep and Matt Damon were among the stars singing Tucci and Blunt's praises at the ceremony. The two "Devil Wears Prada" actors are also in-laws since Tucci married Blunt's sister, Felicity in 2012.

video:video.video:title: EU-Mercosur trade deal becomes provisionally effective, boosts hopes and concerns for 720 million
video:video.video:description: The long-awaited trade deal between South American bloc Mercosur and the European Union takes effect Friday, at least provisionally.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 24-30 April 2026

video:video.video:title: Clashes erupt with police as people in Manila march to the US Embassy on May Day
video:video.video:description: Workers in the Philippines clashed with police near the US Embassy in the capital Manila on Friday during their Labor Day protest. (AP video by Joeal Calupitan)

video:video.video:title: A citizen campaign returns iconic kiwi birds to New Zealand's capital after century-long absence
video:video.video:description: The kiwi, New Zealand’s sacred national bird, vanished from the hills around Wellington more than a century ago, but now the capital's residents are waging an improbable citizen campaign to return the endangered flightless birds to the city.
